Since the beginning, the United States has tried to impose on the country a military and neo-colonial type administration, controlling South Korea politically, culturally and economically. In 1946, to try to legitimize their military administration, they brought from the United States that would be the first dictator of the future puppet republic: Syngman Rhee (or Ri Sin Man). Syngman Rhe was a historically pro-American figure, which had been exiled in the United States in 1904, and was later appointed as president of a self-proclaimed "Democratic Assembly of South Korea", in 1947, would be renamed "Interina Legislative Assembly".
During a certain period, the United States the Soviet Union held several talks to establish a solution to the problem of the division of Korea. According to journalist Anna Louise Strong:
“For two years, these conversations only contributed to increasing bitterness. The Americans insisted on including in the Provisional Government the pro-Japanese collaborators and the exiles who returned. The Russians refused. The Russians insisted on including representatives of trade unions, peasant organizations and similar organizations. The US didn't want to hear about it.
Finally, the Soviets ended up proposing that both countries withdraw from Korea – which was fulfilled by the Soviet party in December 1948 – a proposal obviously rejected by the American imperialists.” [1]
In 1948, the United States promoted a farsighted electoral process, which would later culminate in the founding of the “Republic of Korea”, a puppet state controlled by the United States. The United States, still in 1947, took advantage of its hegemony in the United Nations to approve a resolution that would ensure the election. In this resolution, the so-called United Nations Temporary Committee for Korea emerged, which would be responsible for organizing the electoral farce. The northern part of the Korean peninsula obviously did not take part in the process.
The holding of separate elections was the subject of intense recourse by broad sectors of the Korean masses, which saw to the extent an attempt to perpetuate the division of the peninsula. Even before the elections were held, there was already the understanding that the United States and the South Korean reactionaries aimed at holding elections of such a kind. Throughout the territory of South Korea, revolts against U.S. imperialism broke out.
On May 1, 1947, vast sections of the population of Jeju Island went out to the streets to denounce manoeuvres to hold separate elections. The forces of repression fired against the unarmed population, resulting in the death of about six people. Because it is an island situated south – therefore a separate territory from the mainland part of Korea – Jeju had its own political dynamics. According to historian Bruce Cumings:
" Jeju's effective political leadership, until early 1948, was exercised by powerful left-wing popular committees, which first emerged in August 1945, and continued to operate under American occupation (1945–1948). The American occupation preferred to ignore Jeju, rather than to give reason to the committees; that pointed out a formal continental leadership, but let the people of the island manage their own affairs.” [2]
The manoeuvres of the South Korean reactionaries and the American imperialists to set up a separate country, by carrying out far-reaching elections, triggered a deep hatred in the population of Jeju, which led an armed struggle against US imperialism and its puppets. Largely run by the South Korean Labour Party, the island's masses launched armed confrontations, set fire to voting centers and police stations. The working class carried out important strikes, which drew attention for their radicality.
To suppress the demonstrations of the Jeju population, the American imperialists recruited for dirty work the “services” of the so-called “North Youth League”, paramilitary organization of fascist type, formed in its great measure by reactionary elements that escaped from North Korea. The North Youth League was known for its cruelty to the population. So the historian George Katsiaficas said: “These unpaid murderers lived from the looting of war, raping and murdering from village to village, subjecting the island’s native defenses – particularly women – to torture and humiliation.” [3]
The People's Levant of Jeju was one of the first major revolts of the South Korean people against the oppression of US imperialism and South Korean reactionaries. The People's Levant of Jeju was drowned in a sea of blood, leaving between 30,000 and 60,000 dead, with about 40,000 people seeking exile in Japan. At this time table, there are other important uprisings that can also be remembered, such as the Yo Su Levant. In the port city of Yo Su, a contingent of troops waiting to repress the people of Jeju refused to serve as a cannon bush in the massacre of their countrymen, and rebel against the government, passing to the armed struggle. As we can see, the South Korean people, from the beginning, resisted bravely against the machinations of American imperialism. Only after several repressive campaigns and a real massacre against the population, the Republic of Korea – puppet state until the present time in the southern part of the peninsula – could be founded. During the Korean War, numerous massacres would also be committed by the puppet regime in collusion with US imperialism, so that we could write dozens of articles about each of them.

The first stage of the Republic of Korea was commanded by Syngman Rhee. After the fraudulent elections held on May 10, 1948, Syngman Rhee would rule South Korea from this year to 1960. During the time he was in power, Syngman Rhee headed the country with an iron hand, chasing thousands of patriots who fought against the domination of American imperialism. One of the most emblematic cases of political repression against pro-reunification leadership was the arrest (1956) and execution (1959) of Cho Pong Am, who had been a candidate for the presidency of South Korea by the Progressive Party and received two million votes in the elections held in 1956. Cho Pong Am, like most political opponents to the Marionete regime, would be accused of “Spy of North Korea”.
From the economic point of view, the country was also placed under full dominion and dependence on the US financial “auxiliary” which in 1953 represented 14% of the country’s Gross National Product, and which in 1957 would reach approximately 23%. A significant part of this amount stood in the pockets of a new class of bureaucratic capitalists that formed, as well as the correligionaries of Syngman Rhee and his flock. Corruption reached gigantic levels and contributed to increasing popular dissatisfaction against the regime, which never enjoyed real legitimacy among the popular masses. On March 11, 1960, Masan's population goes to the streets to protest the fraudulent elections held by Syngman Rhee. On March 15, 1960, such protests spread to other cities in South Korea and are violently repressed. The repression against the movement only caused the mass rebellion to increase in adherence. In Seoul, protesters surrounded and set fire to government buildings, the Liberal Party (which was in the government) and the Anti-Communist Youth Association of the Republic of Korea. Although the demonstrations initially directed against the Syngman Rhee government, there was a strong anti-imperialist feeling behind the student's motivations. The U.S. State Department, hypocritically, was aware of having the country’s image linked to the government of Syngman Rhee, published a note requiring the South Korean government to take “necessary and effective actions to protect the democratic right of expression, assembly and freedom of the press,” as well as having “criticized” the government’s repressive measures.
Using a tactic that would be repeated in other episodes – such as in the People's Levant of Gwangju of 1980 –, the United States sought to pass to the South Korean people and international public opinion an image of "distancing" in relation to the Syngman Rhee government, as if the current reactionary regime in South Korea was not itself a Yankee creation. As noted by an editorial of the People’s Diary, published on April 25, 1960, “without the North American bayonets, the Syngman Rhee regime would not have lasted a day at all.” Syngman Rhee eventually resigned, ending his unfortunate U.S. lacquering trajectory in an exile in Hawaii.

After the fall of Syngman Rhee, South Korea lived a very short period of bourgeois “democracy”. The administration of the puppet state passed into the hands of more liberal sectors of the South Korean ruling classes, the state was reorganized into parliamentary bases and Chang Myon was elected Prime Minister of the country. On 16 May 1961, Chang Myon was the victim of a coup d'état led by reactionary Pak Chung Hee. The coup inaugurates a new phase in South Korea's political life, which would also be marked by the brutal exploitation of the workers and repression of the mass movements, especially those of a revolutionary character. Pak Chung Hee establishes a military dictatorship of fascist type.
Pak Chung Hee was a former Japanese collaborator. During his youth, he joined the Imperial Japanese Army and studied at the Tokyo Military Academy. His Japanese name was Okamoto Minoru. Still at the beginning of his government, he installed a so-called “revolutionary court” which was in charge of arresting and executing some names from the previous period. The coup d'état perpetrated by Pak Chung Hee sought to present itself to the masses as the real culmination of the spirit of the April 1960 revolts that overthrew Syngman Rhee. In the beginning, he adopted a hostile stance to the so-called chaebols, South Korean state conglomerates that had begun to thrive still time of Syngman Rhee, thanks to his policy of “import substitution”. Chaebols were seen by South Korean public opinion as sources of corruption. Pak Chung Hee, along with his political collaborator Kim Jong Pil, would create KCIA, an anti-communist espionage agency, which would be known for international kidnappings of South Korean teachers and students living abroad. Promotes an open hostility policy against the socialist countries. The American imperialists assessed that under Pak Chung Hee, Korea would be advancing correctly.
With the Vietnam War, the United States' ability to finance the South Korean economy is weakened (at the time 80% of fixed capital investments came from the United States). Thus, Par Chung Hee proposed, with Yankee support, the normalization of relations and a greater approximation with Japanese imperialism. In March 1964, in front of the negotiations between Japan and South Korea, the South Korean popular masses again stand up, openly shouting “Death to Japanese imperialism!”.
In June 1964, the reactionary government declared martial law and the United States Combined Forces Command authorized the displacement of two combat divisions to suppress the protests. At the same time, the puppet government approves the sending of South Korean soldiers to fight alongside the Yankee invaders in the Vietnam War. Chun Doo Hwan, who would become a central figure in the suppression of the Popular Levant of Gwangju, served as commander of a division of the South Korean puppet army in Vietnam. Participation in the Vietnam conflict was also an opportunity for South Korean reactionaries to profit from the sufferings and massacres of the Vietnamese people. South Korean companies of supply transport, civil construction and entertainment obtained gigantic profits during this period.
To deepen its reactionary domination under the puppet political regime of South Korea, Pak Chung-hee promotes in October 1972 an auto coup, dissolving the National Assembly and drafting a new constitution, which would be known as the Yushin Constitution. The constitution of Yushin represents an even greater closure of a regime that was already brutally dictatorial and fascist. The “new era”, strongly inspired by the Meiji Restoration period in Japan, would raise the levels of exploitation of the South Korean working class at alarming levels.
At the time when he served the Imperial Japanese army, Pak Chung Hee received much ideological influence that would form his worldview for a model of social organization strongly based on hierarchy, strong state control, nationalism and anti-communism. From that time on, the Chaebols begin to despont as the vertebral column of the South Korean economy, which ensured that the most significant sector of the South Korean capitalists decisively supported the regime.
Against the intensification of capitalist exploitation, the workers were seeking ways to organize themselves and fight for their rights. Reporting the extenuating working days to which they were submitted – officially labor law provided 8 hours of workday, something almost never respected –, the workers began sending letters to the most diverse government agencies. Their demands were met with contempt or pure and simple repression. In 1970 – two years before the dictatorial regime of Yushin – Chun Tae Il, a young worker activist, would commit suicide by firing his own body, aiming to draw attention to the problem of the violation of labor rights in South Korea. KCIA exercised a strict control against trade union activity in the country, allowing only the functioning of the trade union center aligned with the regime.
In 1974, the government announced that it had defeated an alleged joint conspiracy with North Korea for the overthrow of the government, organized by a fictional “People’s Revolutionary Party”. Following this episode, thousands of people were arrested and tortured by the regime. The government organized a pharass trial of eight leaders, who allegedly belonged to this group, and would later be executed in 1975. It would later prove that this episode was a farce set up by the reactionary regime.
The number of protests and revolts against the government grew as it was taking more reactionary and repressive forms. Among important sectors of the urban petty-bourgeois grew discontent against the regime.
Given the systematic repression and annihilation of the revolutionary communist opposition, new ideologies and movements begin to direct the opposition struggles against the regime. These movements reflected the aspirations of a sector of the middle bourgeoisie and the petty-bourgeois, excluded from representation within the pro-imperialist regime. Already heavily influenced by Western and distant bourgeois ideas of socialism and communism, this group sought more voice and political participation. At the same time, numerous workers, students and women organizations would be formed in South Korean territory.
In 1979, new mass protests shake South Korea again. A new crisis opens within the regime, and Pak Chung Hee would be murdered by a KCIA member. Nowadays, many bourgeois economists and sociologists consider the reactionary and anti-people regime of Pak Chung Hee as responsible for the “economic miracle” of South Korea, and the policies implemented in this period are presented as “model” for developing countries. At this time, Pak Chung Hee came to power speaking of “economic self-sufficiency” and deepened the implementation of a import replacement program.
Shortly after the assassination of Pak Chung Hee, Chun Doo Hwan, reactionary element, leads another coup and gives rise to a new military regime in South Korea. In 1980, he would be the South Korean fascist chief, chiefly responsible for the crimes against humanity committed in the city of Gwangju during the same year.

As we speak, the present date of May 18, 2020 marks the 40th anniversary of the Gwangju Levant. This important event marked the beginning of the crisis of the military dictatorship sponsored by US imperialism, exerting a powerful influence on the struggle of the South Korean popular masses. The Levant was brutally repressed by the South Korean army, leaving deep marks in South Korean society. The degree of combativity demonstrated by the working class and the students of the city is of great inspiration not only for the democratic and progressive movements of Asia, but also in other parts of the world.
The origin of the Popular Levant of Gwangju dates back to massive student demonstrations, which began in Seoul, May 14, and soon expanded to other regions of South Korea, such as Busan and Gwangju. The students protested against the military dictatorship, claimed the democratization of South Korean society and the end of martial law. University campuses across the country have been occupied. Feeling the pressures of the government and concerned about the consequences of the demonstrations, the student leaders canceled new street acts. The government did not go back to its opposition and sent the army to various regions of the country, including Gwangju, where the students had occupied the National University of Cheonam. On May 16, students at Gwangju held demonstrations in front of the Provincial Palace, which was renamed the “Praça da Democracia” and left in a torch parade.
On May 17, many leaders of the Gwangju movement were arrested, as the army had already shifted its intelligence to act in the city. At national level, several political leaders were arrested. Authorized by Washington, General John Wickman had released the use of paratrooper forces, which were displaced by orders from Chun Doo Hwan to Gwangju. Upon arriving in Gwangju, the troops terrorized and humiliated the city's population.
On May 18, reacting to the violations and repression that were victims, protests in Gwangju acquire a more radicalized character and evolve into open clashes against the army. Students began to widely use molotov cocktails, organizing teams responsible for their production and pitches during protests. People of the most varied social strata begin to spontaneously join the demonstrations. When the proletariat entered the scene, the military reacted violently, indiscriminately shooting the people who participated in the protests. Bus and taxi drivers also took part in the movement, using their vehicles as protesters' shields, to the extent that piles of bodies were forming in the streets and the rescue of people was almost impossible, due to the frenetic shots fired by the military. People reacted with what they had at their fingertips: stones, baseball bats, iron bars, bamboo, etc. On May 19, the military began to use flamethrowers against protesters, incinerating dozens of people.
On May 20, a mutilated body is found, which contributed to further enraging the Gwangju population. Thousands of people gather in the city centre and sing the song “Our desire is reunification”. The march of the protesters gathered about two hundred thousand people and again were received with indiscriminate repression. At a certain time, the protesters formed action groups that took the National Guard facilities, and several prisoners were released. Repression continued violently.
On May 21, the struggle intensifies with the entrance of the workers of the Asia Motors factory. Many residents of Gwangju approached the factory to ask the workers to engage in the struggle. Factory workers accepted the request, stopped production and provided various vehicles for protesters. About 414 military vehicles were donated to protesters. Then a column of workers, driving the vehicles they had produced, arrives in Gwangju to participate in the uprising. At a certain time, protesters formed action groups that took the National Guard facilities and several prisoners were released. Plant workers, fishermen and miners from localities near Gwangju also went to the city, bringing with them weapons and ammunition.
Popular militias – called the “Civil Army” – took over the city, expelling the military, playing a role of “leadership” of the uprising. With the military outside the city, Gwangju became a mume kind of “commune”, with the emergence of quite embryonic forms of new social relations. The spirit of solidarity that was created in society during the days of the uprising is somewhat commented and analyzed in studies on events in Gwangju. Kim Il Sung, then president of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (North Korea), said the People's Levant of Gwangju “was the first massive uprising of the world after the Paris Commune.” [4]
We need to emphasize that the Popular Levant of Gwangju, although it was classified by the South Korean military as a “Communist armed council organized by North Korea”, had in spontaneity one of its main brands. As always, the anti-communism flag was used to justify the massacre and attacks on the masses.
On May 27, the government shifts the special troops stationed in the Demilitarized Zone of Korea (front with the DPRK) to occupy the city of Gwangju and militarily crush the armed resistance of the popular militias formed by the students and workers.

The displacement of troops to the city of Gwangju was approved by General John Wickman, then commander of the Combined Command Forces of the Republic of Korea and the United States. U.S. imperialism’s political and ideologists gave public statements “condemning” any violation of the rights of protesters, while, under the cloths, they conspired and gave authorizations to the reactionary military regime. The gigantic majority of the armaments, ammunition and vehicles used in the repression against the people of the city were provided to the South Korean puppet state by US imperialism. The repression of the Popular Levant was also endorsed by Jimmy Carter, then President of the United States, Zbigniew Brzezinski, National Security Advisor and Richard Holbrooke, Assistant Secretary of State. The great concern of the ideologists of imperialism was to keep the “stability” of the fascist regime and, as it could not fail to be, to fight the ghost of communism. Jimmy Carter, during his electoral campaign, defended the idea of withdrawing U.S. troops stationed in the Korean peninsula, but went to history as a character who collaborated with one of the bloodiest episodes committed by the South Korean regime and imperialism.

As we spoke earlier, President Kim Il Sung came to compare the Popular Levant of Gwangju with the Paris Commune. In fact, there are several similarities between the two events. The two emerged as urban armed insurrections, which established new mechanisms of popular participation and dismantled the mechanisms and repressive forces of the bourgeois state. Although in the case of the Paris Commune the workers' character was much more emphatic, in Gwangju the protests also had an essential participation of the workers, who even introduced a more combative and radicalized dynamic to the movement.
The differences lie in the fact that, politically, the movement in Gwangju is much more “confident” and spontaneous than the Paris Commune. A participant of the movement in Gwangju affirmed that many participants of the Popular Levant had the naive hope that the United States would come to save students, which demonstrates a certain incomprehension of the international political dynamics and the very situation in which they were inserted. Another fact that differentiates Gwangju from the Paris Commune is the fact that the first had an even more incipient duration than the second. The People's Levant of Gwangju lasted only ten days, and despite having instituted a civil committee that began to deliberate on the political affairs of the city, disbarred the repressive apparatus of the bourgeois state for a new military structure emerged from the people, could still not be considered a new power in the correct meaning of the term, given the very embryonic character of these new organizational forms. The events in Gwangju reveal, once again, that without a movement whose main core is the working class, and that besides, it is armed with a correct guiding ideology, greater victories are unsustainable. However, we cannot deny the historical importance of Gwangju Popular Lift and the historical experience of the South Korean popular masses. The Gwangju People's Lift showed that the enemy's forces can be faced with decision. The Levant also denounced the true character of the reactionary military regime and US imperialism. The example of the thousands of fighters who fought against reactionary oppression served as a boost to the overthrow of the fascist military dictatorship in 1987.

[1] Anna Louise Strong. In North Korea: First-Eye Report. Soviet Russia Today, New York: 1949
[2] Bruce Cummings. The Korean War – A History. The Modern Library, New York: 2011
[3] George Katsiafikas. Asia’s Unknown Uprisings – Volume I: South Korean Social Movements in the 20th Century. PM Press, Oakland: 2012
[4] Kim Il Sung. Selected Works – Volume 35. Foreign Language Press, Pyongyang: 1989
Master in Philosophy Marxist by the Normal University of Beijing
en_subtitle: On May 18, 1980, the heroic South Korean people took weapons to oppose the slaughter of the military-fascist regime of Chun Doo Hwan, sponsored by Yankee imperialism

title: Felipe Neto rebate Whindersson Nunes após ataques contra Janja e Lula: "diminuir um pouco a lombra e abrir um maldito livro"

content:

Já com o presidente Lula, ele compartilhou uma postagem de ações estabelecidas pelo governo e disse: “Vai pegar gelo babão”, dando a entender que o presidente não trabalha. 
Felipe então disparou: “Nada contra, mas de vez em quando é bom diminuir um pouco a lombra e abrir um maldito livro”.
O youtuber que tem promovido diversas ações de socorro no RS também fez uma postagem divulgando ações estratégicas do governo Lula no estado. ]

title: Mensagem do vocalista da banda gaúcha “Nenhum de Nós” denunciando fake news sobre tragédia no RS viraliza nas redes (vídeo)
subtitle: "Tem muitas pessoas que estão desumanizando o povo gaúcho. São pessoas que pedem para não politizar e politizam a discussão", desabafou
content:
- Thedy Correa, escritor e vocalista da banda Nenhum de Nós, alertou em sua participação no programa “Altas Horas”, exibido na TV Globo, para as fake news, que estão gerando um ambiente caótico e atrapalham o processo de resgate no Rio Grande do Sul. 
"Tem muitas pessoas que estão desumanizando o povo gaúcho. São pessoas que pedem para não politizar e politizam a discussão. Tem pessoas espalhando fake news que estão prejudicando as contribuições, o socorro. Isso nada mais é que desumanizar as pessoas", disse.
"Se a gente não levar isso a sério, cada um de nós aqui vai ter que enfrentar isso logo, logo. Prestem atenção que isso pode acontecer com vocês e a gente precisa, de uma maneira ou de outra, parar com o negacionismo", acrescentou.
en_title: Message from the vocalist of the gaucho band “No of Us” denouncing fake news about tragedy in the RS viraliza on networks (video)
en_content:
- Thedy Correa, writer and vocalist of the band None of Us, warned in their participation in the program “Altas Horas”, displayed on TV Globo, for the fake news, which are generating a chaotic environment and hinder the rescue process in Rio Grande do Sul.
"There are many people who are dehumanizing the Gaucho people. They are people who ask not to politicize and politicize the discussion. It has people spreading fake news that are hurting contributions, help. This is nothing more than dehumanizing people," he said.
"If we don't take it seriously, each of us here will have to face it soon, soon. Pay attention that this can happen to you and we need, in one way or another, to stop with negationism", he added.
en_subtitle: "There are many people who are dehumanizing the Gaucho people. They are people who ask not to polite and politeize the discussion,"

title: Cantanhêde compara "drama" que viveu com vítimas da tragédia no RS: "roubaram as minhas joias no Natal, foi doloridíssimo"
subtitle: Jornalista foi detonada nas redes pela fala “sem noção”; confira a repercussão e o vídeo
content:
A jornalista Eliane Cantanhêde supostamente tentou praticar o gesto de empatia com a população gaúcha, vítima das enchentes que assolam o estado, mas acabou sendo detonada nas redes pela falta de noção. Em comentário na GloboNews, ela decidiu comparar o "drama" que viveu com vítimas da tragédia no RS: “você se põe no lugar dessas pessoas, roubaram minhas joias no Natal de 2023, foi doloridíssimo”.
A fala foi imediatamente criticada. 
“Uma pessoa sem noção, com um comentário mais sem noção ainda”, disse a internauta Cídya Mara.
“Eu estava vendo ao vivo e duvidei do que eu tinha escutado. Fiquei achando que eu estava maluca, pois ela falou isso mesmo e depois falou da tragédia do RS. Como se fosse algo similar, como se as perdas fossem iguais e pudessem estar na mesma frase”, indicou a internauta Fernanda Sayão.
en_title: Cantanhêde compares "drama" who lived with victims of tragedy in the RS: "they stole my jewels at Christmas, it was dolorious"
en_content:
The journalist Eliane Cantanhêde allegedly tried to practice the gesture of empathy with the gaúcha population, victim of the floods that plague the state, but ended up being detonated on the nets for lack of notion. In a comment on GloboNews, she decided to compare the "drama" that lived with victims of the tragedy in the RS: "You put yourself in the place of these people, stole my jewels at Christmas 2023, was dolorious".
The speech was immediately criticized.
“An unnoticed person, with a more unnotioned comment yet”, said the boarder Cídya Mara.
“I was seeing live and I doubted what I had heard. I was thinking I was crazy, because she talked about it and then talked about the RS tragedy. As if it were something similar, as if the losses were equal and could be in the same sentence ", said the internauta Fernanda Sayão.
en_subtitle: Journalist was detonated in the networks by the speech “no notion”; check the repercussion and video

title: “Hoje nós temos um governo por nós, olhando a nação brasileira e atendendo a emergência do nosso povo”, diz Maria do Rosário
subtitle: Deputada critica governo anterior e destaca comprometimento do atual com a soberania e as questões ambientais
content:
A deputada federal Maria do Rosário participou do programa Boa Noite 247, onde discutiu diversos temas relacionados à política nacional e às questões ambientais. Durante a entrevista, a deputada destacou a importância de um governo comprometido com a soberania nacional e criticou a gestão anterior.
"O projeto de soberania foi muito atacado por essa lógica de perseguição política dos setores democráticos da esquerda brasileira, que em cada momento tem sofrido todos os ataques. Isso culminou, de um lado, naquele momento trágico para nós, quando a presidenta Dilma foi afastada do poder sem nenhum crime. É algo bárbaro, o que aconteceu contra a nação brasileira, contra o nosso povo," afirmou a deputada.
Maria do Rosário também mencionou a prisão do presidente Lula e seu impacto nas eleições de 2018. "A prisão do presidente Lula, para ele não poder participar do processo eleitoral de 2018, nos traz uma elite, que não se sente bem quando o Brasil está bem, que não olha quando o país inteiro vai bem, porque, ela prefere a desigualdade," disse.
A deputada fez duras críticas às ações do governo anterior, que, segundo ela, prejudicaram empresas públicas e o meio ambiente. "Temos no Brasil hoje em disputa, de um lado quem não tem compromisso com a soberania nacional, que vestiu a bandeira do Brasil nas costas durante um período mas ao mesmo tempo queria privatizar os Correios, atacou a Petrobras, atacou todas as empresas públicas, privatizou a Eletrobrás, atacou o meio ambiente," declarou.
Ela também comentou sobre os desafios ambientais enfrentados pelo Rio Grande do Sul e sua ligação com a destruição ambiental mais ampla no país. "O que nós estamos vivendo aqui no RS também é ligado à Amazônia, o que aconteceu no Pantanal. Nós somos um estado que vai se acostumando a lidar com essas expressões, nós temos um rio, que passa por cima estado, com todas as correntes de ar que vêm da Amazônia, além daquelas que vêm da Patagônia e que se entrechocam de uma forma a produzir efeitos climáticos extremamente complexos e com continuidade e isso tudo tem a ver com a destruição que o país estava vivendo, não deu tempo ainda de recuperar tudo isso," explicou.
Maria do Rosário expressou otimismo com a ajuda do governo federal ao Rio grande do Sul, seu estado: "Eu penso, que essa situação que a gente está vivendo no Rio Grande do Sul já mostra de imediato um aspecto, hoje nós temos um governo, nós temos um governo por nós, temos um governo olhando a nação brasileira e pensando em atender a emergência do nosso povo," finalizou.
Assista:
en_title: “Today we have a government for us, looking at the Brazilian nation and attending the emergence of our people”, says Maria do Rosário
en_content:
Congresswoman Maria do Rosário participated in the Good Night 247, where she discussed various topics related to national policy and environmental issues. During the interview, she highlighted the importance of a government committed to national sovereignty and criticized previous management.
" The project of sovereignty was greatly attacked by this logic of political persecution of the democratic sectors of the Brazilian left, which at every moment has suffered all attacks. This culminated, on the one hand, at that tragic moment for us, when President Dilma was removed from power without any crime. It is a barbarous thing, what happened against the Brazilian nation, against our people," said the MP.
Maria do Rosário also mentioned the arrest of President Lula and his impact on the 2018 elections. "The arrest of President Lula, so he can not participate in the election process 2018, brings us an elite, which does not feel well when Brazil is well, which does not look when the whole country goes well, because, she prefers inequality," he said.
She criticized the previous government's actions, which, according to her, undermined public companies and the environment. "We have in Brazil today in dispute, on the one hand who has no commitment to national sovereignty, who wore the flag of Brazil on the back for a period but at the same time wanted to privatize the Post Office, attacked Petrobras, attacked all public companies, privatized Eletrobrás, attacked the environment," he said.
She also commented on the environmental challenges faced by Rio Grande do Sul and its connection to the wider environmental destruction in the country. "What we are living here in the RS is also connected to the Amazon, what happened in the Pantanal. We are a state that will be accustomed to dealing with these expressions, we have a river, that goes above state, with all the air currents coming from the Amazon, beyond those that come from Patagonia and that intershock in a way to produce extremely complex weather effects and with continuity and that all has to do with the destruction that the country was living, did not give time yet to recover all this," explained.
Maria do Rosário expressed optimism with the help of the federal government to Rio Grande do Sul, its state: "I think, that this situation that we are living in Rio Grande do Sul already shows immediately one aspect, today we have a government, we have a government for us, we have a government looking at the Brazilian nation and thinking about meeting the emergence of our people," he said.
Watch:
en_subtitle: Deputed criticizes previous government and highlights current commitment to sovereignty and environmental issues

title: “Ninguém conseguiu pautar a mídia como Alexandre de Moraes conseguiu”, diz jurista
subtitle: Juiz de Direito comenta mandatos de ministros do TSE, e processos envolvendo o ex-presidente Bolsonaro no STF
content:
O desembargador no Tribunal de Justiça de São Paulo e ex-presidente da Associação Juízes para a Democracia, Marcelo Semer, concedeu entrevista ao programa Boa Noite 247, onde abordou temas como os mandatos no TSE da Ministra Carmen Lúcia e do Ministro Alexandre de Moraes, além dos processos judiciais envolvendo o ex-presidente Jair Bolsonaro, no STF.
Questionado sobre o que espera do mandato da Ministra Carmen Lúcia e do final do mandato do Ministro Alexandre de Moraes, Semer afirmou: "Ela já foi do tribunal eleitoral, não vejo sobressaltos nisso, talvez, não seja tão politicamente montado quanto ao do ministro Alexandre de Moraes. Acho que ninguém consegue competir com Alexandre nesse quesito, porque ele tem uma origem muito maior na política, consegue medir a temperatura política e a temperatura da mídia como nenhum outro conseguiu. Para pautar a mídia, nenhum outro ministro do Supremo tinha essa facilidade. Enfim, mas também enfrentou uma conjuntura inédita na história brasileira, em termos eleitorais."
Sobre a atuação do Tribunal Superior Eleitoral (TSE), Semer disse: "Não dá para dizer que o TSE tenha sido negligente, ao contrário, julgou os processos de Bolsonaro com bastante rapidez e fluidez. Acho também que a mesma coisa aconteceu na decisão no caso Dallagnol. Então penso que o TSE seja um tribunal garantista. O tribunal vai mudar bastante, principalmente pelas saídas, quando mudam os advogados, quando mudam os Ministros do Supremo, não sabemos o que vem pela frente. Aparentemente, o que vem pela frente é um tribunal um pouco mais conservador com a presidente Carmen Lúcia."
Sobre os processos envolvendo Bolsonaro e seus colaboradores no STF, Semer declarou: "Me parece que, com relação ao processo que envolve o Bolsonaro e os seus colaboradores, não vejo outro lugar para ser julgado que não seja o Supremo. Não faz sentido. O Supremo realizou de novo a sua jurisprudência. No que diz respeito a Bolsonaro, eu acho muito razoável, porque um ex-presidente, que na presidência atentou contra a democracia por meio de um golpe, não vejo outro lugar onde possa ser julgado se não no Supremo."
Assista:
en_title: " Nobody managed to guide the media as Alexandre de Moraes succeeded ", says jurist
en_content:
The disembargator at the Court of Justice of São Paulo and former president of the Judges Association for Democracy, Marcelo Semer, gave an interview to the Good Night program 247, where he addressed topics such as the mandates at the TSE of Minister Carmen Lucia and Minister Alexandre de Moraes, in addition to judicial proceedings involving former President Jair Bolsonaro, in the STF.
Asked about what awaits the mandate of Minister Carmen Lucia and the end of the term of Minister Alexandre de Moraes, Semer said: "She's already been from the electoral court, I don't see any stress on that, perhaps, not as politically mounted as that of Minister Alexandre de Moraes. I don't think anyone can compete with Alexander in this matter, because he has a much greater origin in politics, he can measure the political temperature and the media temperature as no other has achieved. To guide the media, no other minister of the Supreme had this ease. Finally, but also faced an unprecedented conjuncture in Brazilian history, in electoral terms. "
On the performance of the Superior Electoral Court (TSE), Semer said: "It cannot be said that TSE was negligent, on the contrary, judged Bolsonaro's processes very quickly and fluidly. I also think the same thing happened in the decision in the Dallagnol case. So I think TSE is a guarantee court. The court will change a lot, especially the exits, when they change the lawyers, when they change the ministers of the Supreme, we don't know what's going on ahead. Apparently, what's ahead is a slightly more conservative court with President Carmen Lucia. "
About the processes involving Bolsonaro and his employees in the Supreme Court, Semer stated: "It seems to me that with regard to the process involving Bolsonaro and his collaborators, I see no other place to be judged not to be the Supreme. It doesn't make sense. The Supreme Court held its jurisprudence again. As far as Bolsonaro is concerned, I find it very reasonable, because an ex-president, who in the presidency looked at democracy through a coup, I see no other place where it can be judged if not in the Supreme. "

en_subtitle: Law Judge comments mandates of TSE ministers, and processes involving former President Bolsonaro in the Supreme Court

title: "STF teve de se adaptar para se defender", analisa Lenio Streck em entrevista
subtitle: A evolução defensiva do STF diante de ataques: análise de Lenio Streck
content:
A análise corrente hoje, de que o Supremo Tribunal Federal extrapola suas atribuições e estende seu poder sobre os demais agentes da República, ignora o fato de que a corte precisou se defender de ataques criminosos, em um ambiente político que privilegiava a desestabilização e o caos.Esse diagnóstico foi feito pelo advogado e professor 
“Em um ecossistema político-criminal, as espécies vão se adaptando, e o Supremo foi ganhando anticorpos para se defender.”
O cenário incluía um tuíte de um general do Exército ameaçando os ministros na véspera de um julgamento, desfile de tanques de guerra pelas ruas da capital do país e o presidente da República chamando um magistrado do STF de canalha durante as comemorações do 7 de Setembro, só para ficar em alguns exemplos.
Não é que o Supremo não tenha sua parcela de responsabilidade. Nos primeiros momentos, o tribunal ajudou a turbinar a “lava jato” e o lavajatismo. Segundo a análise de Streck, no embate entre o então ministro Teori Zavascki e o então juiz Sergio Moro, a primeira instância se sobrepôs ao STF.
Em 2016, a corte mudou seu entendimento sobre a presunção de inocência, o que viabilizou a prisão de Lula. “Todos esses elementos foram fortalecendo a ‘lava jato’, esses movimentos anti-Supremo. E, por incrível que pareça, o Supremo, naquele momento, vitaminou a ‘lava jato’.”
Os desdobramentos dessas medidas, depois da eleição de Jair Bolsonaro para a Presidência, fizeram o Supremo perceber que sua própria existência, para não dizer a das instituições, estava sob ameaça.
Até o teste final das eleições de 2022, e depois o episódio de terrorismo no 8 de janeiro de 2023, a corte foi ganhando cada vez mais protagonismo pelas medidas adotadas em sua própria defesa.
Mesmo o inquérito aberto de ofício era essencial, avalia Streck, diante da inação da PGR, então chefiada por Raquel Dodge.
“O Supremo defende a Constituição, e nisso está pendurada a democracia. Se o Supremo é atacado, não se defende a Constituição, não tem mais Supremo, não tem Constituição, não tem mais democracia.”
O protagonismo do Supremo, então, é “uma decorrência de todos esses sucessos e insucessos históricos”, analisa Streck. “Não é uma ideia, não é um planejamento, é simplesmente o produto de uma contingência histórica.”
“Se o Ministério Público, por exemplo, tivesse defendido o Supremo naquele momento, não haveria nada disso. Se tivesse entrado com ações contra as pessoas que estavam na frente dos quartéis, cuidado das rádios que todos os dias incentivavam o golpe, nada disso teria acontecido.”
en_title: "STF had to adapt to defend itself", analyzes Lenio Streck in an interview
en_content:
The current analysis today, that the Supreme Court extrapolates its duties and extends its power over the other agents of the Republic, ignores the fact that the court needed to defend itself from criminal attacks, in a political environment that privileged destabilization and chaos. This diagnosis was made by the lawyer and teacher
" In a political-criminal ecosystem, the species will adapt, and the Supreme has been gaining antibodies to defend itself. ”
The scenario included a tweet of an army general threatening ministers on the eve of a trial, a war tank parade on the streets of the country's capital and the president of the Republic calling a scumbag of straw during the 7th September celebrations, just to stay in some examples.
It is not that the Supreme does not have his share of responsibility. In the first moments, the court helped to turbine the “float jet” and the lavage. According to Streck's analysis, in the clash between then-Minister Teori Zavascki and then-Jer Sergio Moro, the first instance overlapped with the Supreme Court.
In 2016, the court changed its understanding of the presumption of innocence, which enabled Lula's arrest. “All of these elements were strengthening the ‘float jet’, these anti-supply movements. And, as incredible as it may seem, the Supreme at that time, vitamined the ‘floor wash’. ”
The unfolding of these measures, after the election of Jair Bolsonaro to the Presidency, made the Supreme realize that his own existence, not to tell the institutions, was under threat.
Until the final test of the 2022 elections, and then the terrorist episode on January 8, 2023, the court was gaining more and more protagonism by the measures adopted in its own defense.
Even the open trade survey was essential, Streck evaluates, in front of the PGR's inaction, then headed by Raquel Dodge.
“The Supreme defends the Constitution, and democracy is hanging in it. If the Supreme is attacked, it is not defended by the Constitution, it has no more Supreme, no Constitution, no more democracy. ”
The protagonism of the Supreme, then, is “a result of all these historical successes and failures”, Streck analyzes. “It’s not an idea, it’s not a planning, it’s simply the product of a historical contingency. ”
“If the Public Prosecutor, for example, had defended the Supreme at that time, there would be nothing of this. If he had come in with actions against the people who were in front of the barracks, watch out for the radios that every day encouraged the coup, none of that would have happened. ”
en_subtitle: The defensive evolution of the Supreme Court in the face of attacks: analysis of Lenio Streck

title: “Moro não foi cassado porque o que preside o direito é a política”, diz Alysson Mascaro
subtitle: Jurista Alysson Mascaro analisa implicações políticas da absolvição de Moro pelo TSE
content:
Em entrevista ao programa Brasil Agora, da TV 247, o filósofo Alysson Mascaro fez uma análise sobre a 
 Mascaro afirmou: "Moro não foi cassado porque o que preside o direito é a política" e acrescentou: "Quando Lula foi condenado por razões absurdas, ele foi condenado pela política – e não pelo direito".
Ao abordar especificamente o papel de Sergio Moro e suas decisões, Mascaro argumentou que "Moro foi salvo porque serviu ao neoliberalismo" e enfatizou que suas ações foram cruciais para a ascensão desse modelo econômico no Brasil. O jurista destacou o papel de Moro no contexto político nacional, afirmando que "Moro é o grande algoz do presidente Lula. Se a ele nada acontece, passa a imagem de que o acordo até a ele chega". Mascaro também apontou para a influência do capital nas instituições brasileiras, declarando que "As instituições não são neutras, nem técnicas, elas são sempre controladas pelo capital nas sociedades capitalistas".
A análise do jurista não se limitou apenas ao passado, mas também especulou sobre o futuro político do país, sugerindo que as recentes decisões judiciais podem indicar a possibilidade de um "acordão" político, que poderia afetar a possibilidade de condenação de Jair Bolsonaro. O jurista sugeriu que as recentes decisões judiciais, como a absolvição de Moro e 
Analisando o posicionamento da esquerda brasileira, Mascaro destacou a falta de análise marxista e a dependência do neoliberalismo. "Falta análise marxista na esquerda brasileira", disse ele, acrescentando: "A esquerda que não aposta na mobilização das massas, é refém do neoliberalismo" e ainda afirmou que "O corno de sempre é a esquerda liberal", sugerindo que, mesmo diante de adversidades, essa ala política não consegue escapar da influência do capital.

title: “Caso apareça envolvimento de sionistas na queda do helicóptero, algumas pessoas falam em terceira guerra mundial”, diz Lejeune
subtitle: Analista Internacional comenta morte do presidente iraniano
content:
Durante uma entrevista no programa Brasil Agora da TV 247, o analista internacional, sociólogo, escritor e professor Lejeune Mirhan comentou sobre a morte do presidente iraniano Ebrahim Raisi em um acidente de helicóptero.
"Não vai haver mudança em relação ao apoio à resistência palestina”. “Eu vi uma declaração do Aiatolá Ali al-Sistani - que não usa o nome Israel, mas entidade sionista - disse que se ficar comprovado alguma ligação da entidade sionista neste episódio vocês não têm ideia de como será a reação do Irã. Ele não levantou suspeita, não apontou o dedo para ninguém, mas já deixou avisado. Algumas pessoas falam em terceira guerra mundial", afirmou Mirhan durante a entrevista.
A morte de Raisi ocorre em um momento de crescente dissidência interna e pressão internacional sobre o Irã, especialmente relacionada ao seu programa nuclear e laços militares com a Rússia. Raisi era considerado um forte candidato a suceder Khamenei, que o havia apoiado em suas principais políticas.
Por sua vez, Israel negou qualquer envolvimento na tragédia. Um oficial israelense, que pediu anonimato, afirmou à imprensa: "Não fomos nós". Rumores sobre o suposto envolvimento do Mossad, agência de inteligência israelense, circularam nas redes sociais, porém, não foram corroborados por evidências concretas.
Assista:
en_title: " If Zionist involvement appears in the helicopter crash, some people speak in the third world war ", says Lejeune
en_content:
During an interview in Brazil Now from TV 247, the international analyst, sociologist, writer and professor Lejeune Mirhan commented on the death of Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi in a helicopter accident.
"There will be no change in support of Palestinian resistance." “I’ve seen a statement from Ayatollah Ali al-Sistani – who doesn’t use the name Israel, but Zionist entity – said that if any connection of the Zionist entity is proven in this episode you have no idea how Iran’s reaction will be. He didn't raise suspicion, he didn't point his finger at anyone, but he already warned. Some people speak in the third world war," Mirhan said during the interview.
Raisi's death occurs at a time of growing internal dissent and international pressure on Iran, especially related to its nuclear program and military ties with Russia. Raisi was considered a strong candidate to succeed Khamenei, who had supported him in his main policies.
In turn, Israel denied any involvement in the tragedy. An Israeli officer, who asked for anonymity, said to the press: "It wasn't us." Rumors about the alleged involvement of the Mossad, Israeli intelligence agency, circulated on social networks, but were not corroborated by concrete evidence.
Watch:
en_subtitle: International Analyst comments the death of Iranian President

title: “Nunca Israel esteve tão isolado”, diz Lejeune Mirhan
subtitle: Analista internacional discute decisão do TPI e cenário geopolítico de massacre na Palestina
content:
Durante sua participação no programa Brasil Agora da TV 247, o analista internacional, sociólogo, escritor e professor Lejeune Mirhan discutiu a recente decisão do Tribunal Penal Internacional (TPI) e suas repercussões no conflito entre Israel e Palestina.
"Esperava-se essa decisão do tribunal já há muito tempo. Acontece que este é um tribunal político. Os teóricos do Direito Internacional já discutiam que este tribunal penal é uma excrescência porque viola as soberanias", declarou Mirhan.
Mirhan também comentou sobre as pressões internacionais que influenciaram a decisão  promotor-chefe do TPI, Karim Khan, para emitir mandados de prisão contra líderes do Hamas e Israel por acusações de crimes de guerra e crimes contra a humanidade: "O promotor Karim Ham cedeu a pressão internacional dos Estados Unidos que dizia que o ideal seria não pedir, mas já que vai pedir tem que pedir também a prisão dos líderes palestinos da resistência."
Além disso, o sociólogo analisou o atual cenário geopolítico, especialmente em relação a Israel: "Netanyahu está perdendo a guerra. Não se mede quem ganha uma guerra ou quem perde pelo número de mortes que eles estão conseguindo produzir. Nunca Israel esteve tão isolado como está desde 7 de outubro. Aquela aura protetora de 'povo escolhido por Deus' ou 'terra prometida por Deus' se quebrou, já não existe mais, a ponto da União Europeia, sob liderança da Ursula Von der Leyen, um governo de extrema direita, estar contra e pedindo cessar-fogo já faz várias semanas."
en_title: “Never has Israel been so isolated,” says Lejeune Mirhan
en_content:
During your participation in the program Brazil Now from TV 247, the international analyst, sociologist, writer and professor Lejeune Mirhan discussed the recent decision of the International Criminal Court (ICT) and its repercussions on the conflict between Israel and Palestine.
" That court decision was expected a long time ago. Turns out this is a political court. International Law theorists have already argued that this criminal court is an excretion because it violates the sovereignty," Mirhan said.
Mirhan also commented on the international pressures that influenced the ICC chief prosecutor Karim Khan's decision to issue arrest warrants against Hamas and Israel leaders on charges of war crimes and crimes against humanity: "Prosecutor Karim Ham gave up the international pressure of the United States that said the ideal would not be to ask, but since it will ask has to also ask for the arrest of Palestinian leaders of resistance. "
In addition, the sociologist analyzed the current geopolitical scenario, especially in relation to Israel: "Netanyahu is losing the war. Do not measure who wins a war or who loses by the number of deaths they are managing to produce. Israel has never been as isolated as it has been since 7 October. That protective aura of 'people chosen by God' or 'land promised by God' broke, no longer exists, to the point of the European Union, under the leadership of Ursula Von der Leyen, an extreme right-wing government, being against and asking cease-fire for several weeks. "
en_subtitle: International analyst discusses ICC decision and geopolitical scene of massacre in Palestine

title: "A absolvição de Moro acendeu uma luz amarela", diz Alfredo Attié
subtitle: Desembargador critica decisão do TSE e alerta sobre retrocesso na jurisprudência eleitoral
content:
Em entrevista ao programa Boa Noite 247, o desembargador Alfredo Attié comentou a recente decisão do Tribunal Superior Eleitoral (TSE) que absolveu o ex-juiz e atual senador Sergio Moro das acusações de abuso de poder econômico durante a campanha eleitoral de 2022. Attié expressou surpresa com o resultado do julgamento e levantou questões sobre a proteção de políticos em detrimento da democracia.
"Eu fiquei surpreso com a absolvição do Moro pelo TSE, imaginei que fossemos seguir a jurisprudência que levou Bolsonaro à ineligibilidade, seguir com os pés no chão," afirmou Attié. Ele criticou a decisão, sugerindo que há uma proteção à política tradicional em detrimento da população. "Estão protegendo a política tradicional e não se protege o próprio povo, a democracia."
Attié também destacou que a lei eleitoral brasileira é clara sobre a inexistência de pré-campanha, apontando que Moro se apresentou como candidato desde o início, o que é vedado pela legislação. "A lei eleitoral é clara, não existe pré-campanha. O Moro desde sempre se apresentou como candidato, isso é vedado pela lei. Foram feitos gastos absurdos, isso é dinheiro público."
O desembargador alertou sobre um possível retrocesso na jurisprudência que anteriormente visava proteger a integridade eleitoral. "Estamos voltando atrás, para a velha jurisprudência que protegia os políticos."
Attié foi enfático ao afirmar que a extrema-direita atua fora da lei e que a justiça não pode permanecer neutra diante de ações criminosas. "A extrema direita é criminosa, ela age fora da lei. E a justiça não pode ficar neutra diante do crime."
Sobre os crimes de 8 de janeiro de 2023, ele criticou a falta de processos contra os mandantes e financiadores dos atos. "Só estão sendo processadas as pessoas que foram para a Praça dos Três Poderes. Os mandantes e os financiadores não aparecem."
Concluindo, Alfredo Attié mencionou que a decisão do TSE sobre Moro é preocupante. "A absolvição de Moro acendeu uma luz amarela."
Assista:
en_title: " The acquittal of Moro lit a yellow light," says Alfredo Attié
en_content:
In an interview with the Good Night program 247, the disembargator Alfredo Attié commented on the recent decision of the Superior Electoral Court (TSE) which acquitted former judge and current Senator Sergio Moro of charges of abuse of economic power during the election campaign of 2022. Attié expressed surprise with the outcome of the trial and raised questions about the protection of politicians to the detriment of democracy.
" I was surprised by the acquittal of Moro by the TSE, I imagined that we would follow the jurisprudence that led Bolsonaro to ineligibility, follow with feet on the ground," Attié said. He criticized the decision, suggesting that there is a protection of traditional policy to the detriment of the population. "They are protecting traditional politics and not protecting the people themselves, democracy. "
Attié also highlighted that the Brazilian electoral law is clear about the lack of pre-campaign, pointing out that Moro presented himself as a candidate from the beginning, which is sealed by the legislation. "The electoral law is clear, there is no pre-campaign. Moro has always presented himself as a candidate, this is sealed by the law. Nonsense expenses have been made, that's public money. "
The disembargator warned about a possible recession in the caselaw which previously sought to protect electoral integrity. "We are turning back to the old jurisprudence that protected politicians. "
Attié was emphatic in asserting that the extreme right acts outside the law and that justice cannot remain neutral in the face of criminal actions. "The extreme right is criminal, she acts out of the law. And justice cannot be neutral in the face of the crime. "
About the crimes of 8 January 2023, he criticized the lack of lawsuits against the agents and funders of the acts. "Only the people who went to the Three Powers Square are being processed. The bosses and the funders don't show up. "
In conclusion, Alfredo Attié mentioned that TSE's decision on Moro is troubling. "The acquittal of Moro lit a yellow light. "
Watch:
en_subtitle: Disembargator criticizes the decision of the TSE and warns of recession in electoral jurisprudence

title: “Leite e Melo, duplinha dinâmica que destruiu a estrutura pública que cuidava da prevenção à tragédia”, diz Kliass
subtitle: Economista Paulo Kliass responsabiliza governador e prefeito de Porto Alegre pela falta de investimentos em prevenção a enchentes no Rio Grande do Sul
content:
Na entrevista ao programa Brasil Agora, da TV 247, o economista Paulo Kliass afirmou que o governador Eduardo Leite e o prefeito Sebastião Melo adotaram uma política neoliberal e negacionista, negligenciando as necessidades de infraestrutura para a prevenção de tragédias climáticas. "Essa duplinha dinâmica, como eu chamo, o Eduardo Leite no governo do estado e o Sebastião Melo na Capital, em Porto Alegre, não fizeram os investimentos necessários por uma questão muito objetiva", declarou o economista.
Ele ressaltou que, desde a primeira grande cheia em 1941, foram necessárias ações contínuas de prevenção que, segundo ele, foram desconsideradas pelos gestores públicos. "Eles sabiam exatamente o que era necessário fazer para prevenir uma questão que, desde a década de 40, quando teve a primeira cheia, não é só um problema da natureza, mas também de políticas responsáveis", afirmou Kliass. 
O economista apontou que políticas como o desmatamento e a priorização do agronegócio e da especulação imobiliária agravaram a situação das enchentes. "A intervenção do ser humano, através de políticas irresponsáveis, desmatamento e concessão da prioridade ao agronegócio, acabou agravando a situação", explicou.
Ele também mencionou que a estrutura estatal e municipal destinada à prevenção foi desmantelada. "Eles destruíram a estrutura do estado e da prefeitura que cuidava disso porque consideravam coisa de 'ambientaloíde', como eles diziam", disse o economista. Segundo Kliass, recursos foram desviados para outras áreas que os gestores consideravam prioritárias.
Em decorrência das chuvas recentes, o Rio Grande do Sul enfrentou enchentes que resultaram em mortes e desabrigados. "Agora veio a desgraça, a tragédia", comentou Kliass. Ele criticou Leite por tentar se esquivar de suas responsabilidades, atribuindo a culpa a questões fiscais. "Eduardo Leite tenta se esquivar para não assumir a sua responsabilidade e tenta jogar genericamente sobre a pauta fiscal. Mas não é verdade", concluiu Kliass.
Assista:
en_title: “Leite e Melo, dynamic duplinha that destroyed the public structure that cared for the prevention of tragedy”, says Kliass
en_content:
In the interview with the program Brasil Agora of TV 247, economist Paulo Kliass stated that Governor Eduardo Leite and Mayor Sebastião Melo adopted a neoliberal and denialist policy, neglecting the infrastructure needs for the prevention of climate tragedies. "This dynamic duplinha, as I call it, Eduardo Leite in the state government and Sebastião Melo in Capital, in Porto Alegre, did not make the necessary investments for a very objective question," said the economist.
He pointed out that, since the first large flood in 1941, continuous prevention actions were needed that, according to him, were disregarded by public managers. "They knew exactly what it was necessary to do to prevent a question that, since the 40s, when it had the first full, is not only a problem of nature, but also of responsible policies," Kliass said.
The economist pointed out that policies such as deforestation and prioritization of agribusiness and real estate speculation aggravated the flood situation. "The intervention of the human being, through irresponsible policies, deforestation and granting priority to agribusiness, ended up aggravating the situation," he explained.
He also mentioned that the state and municipal structure for prevention was dismantled. "They destroyed the structure of the state and the prefecture that looked after it because they considered it 'environment', as they said," said the economist. According to Kliass, resources were diverted to other areas that managers considered priority.
As a result of recent rains, the Rio Grande do Sul faced floods that resulted in deaths and homeless. "Now came the disgrace, the tragedy," said Kliass. He criticized Leite for trying to avoid his responsibilities, assigning the blame to tax issues. "Eduardo Leite tries to avoid taking responsibility and tries to play generically on the fiscal agenda. But it's not true," Kliass concluded.
Watch:
en_subtitle: Economist Paulo Kliass blames governor and mayor of Porto Alegre for the lack of investments in prevention to floods in Rio Grande do Sul

title: “Absolvição abre espaço para Moro tentar o governo do Paraná em 2026”, diz Esmael Morais
subtitle: Decisão do TSE mantém mandato de Moro, abrindo caminho para possível candidatura ao governo do Paraná em 2026, segundo análise de Esmael Morais
content:
O Tribunal Superior Eleitoral (TSE) decidiu, na última terça-feira (21), manter o mandato do senador Sérgio Moro. A decisão foi tomada com o voto decisivo do ministro Nunes Marques, acompanhando o relator Floriano de Azevedo Marques e outros ministros. Moro foi acusado pelo PT e pelo PL de abusos financeiros durante a campanha eleitoral de 2022, mas a maioria do TSE considerou que não havia provas suficientes para cassar seu mandato.
Em entrevista ao programa Boa Noite 247, Esmael Morais, do Blog do Esmael, afirmou que essa absolvição abre caminho para que Moro concorra ao governo do Paraná em 2026. Segundo Morais, “Vamos ter a medida desse acordo nos próximos dias, aí vamos ter a extensão desse acordo.”
Esmael apontou que a candidatura de Moro pode causar tensões entre as elites políticas locais: “Ele está furando a fila, se colocando como candidato das elites tradicionais paranaenses. Isso pode até causar um desarranjo.” Para ele, Moro não possui um projeto político claro para o estado, mas se apoiaria na sua imagem de combate à corrupção: “Ele vai disputar a vaga do Paraná, mas como ele não tem projeto, não tem time, possivelmente ele partirá sobre o fetiche da ‘caça ao corrupto’”.
Esmael Morais também comentou sobre as ambições políticas de Moro além do Paraná: “O mundo político está em polvorosa, ele está furando a fila, mas não é seu objetivo final o Paraná”. Morais acredita que o verdadeiro objetivo de Moro é a presidência da República: “Ele quer chegar ao Palácio do Planalto”. E acrescentou: “Mas o sonho dele é chegar à presidência e ocupar a cadeira do Lula”. Segundo Morais, Moro poderia até surgir como candidato à presidência com o apoio da mídia: “De repente, ele aparece até como candidato a presidente, com apoio da Globo”.
Assista:
en_title: " Absolution opens space for Moro to try the government of Paraná in 2026”, says Esmael Morais
en_content:
The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) decided, last Tuesday (21), to maintain the mandate of Senator Sergio Moro. The decision was taken with the decisive vote of Minister Nunes Marques, following the rapporteur Floriano de Azevedo Marques and other ministers. Moro was accused by the PT and the PL of financial abuses during the election campaign of 2022, but most of the TSE considered that there was not enough evidence to impeach his term.
In an interview with the program Good Night 247, Esmael Morais of the Esmael Blog, said that this absolution opens the way for Moro to run for the government of Paraná in 2026. According to Morais, “We will have the measure of this agreement in the coming days, there we will have the extension of this agreement. ”
Esmael pointed out that Moro's candidacy can cause tensions among local political elites: “He’s lining up, putting himself as a candidate for the traditional Paranaense elites. This can even cause a disarray.” For him, Moro does not have a clear political project for the state, but would support himself in his image of combating corruption: “He will dispute the vacancy of the Paraná, but as he has no project, has no team, possibly he will depart on the fetish of the “fall to the corrupt”.
Esmael Morais also commented on the political ambitions of Moro beyond Parana: “The political world is in octopus, it is piercing the line, but it is not its final goal the Paraná”. Morais believes that Moro's true goal is the presidency of the Republic: “He wants to get to the Palace of the Plateau”. And he added: “But his dream is to reach the presidency and occupy the chair of Lula”. According to Morais, Moro could even appear as a candidate for the presidency with the support of the media: “ Suddenly, he appears as a candidate for president, with support from Globo”.
Watch:
en_subtitle: TSE decision maintains Moro's mandate, opening the way for possible application to the Paraná government in 2026, according to analysis of Esmael Morais

title: “A idiossubjetivação é uma ferramenta usada pelo neoliberalismo para formatar pessoas que naturalizam o absurdo”, diz juiz
subtitle: Juiz Rubens Casara discute em entrevista à TV 247 o impacto do neoliberalismo na formação subjetiva dos indivíduos
content:
O juiz de direito do Tribunal de Justiça do Rio de Janeiro (TJ-RJ) e escritor Rubens Casara discutiu, em entrevista ao programa Giro das Onze, da TV 247, seu livro "A construção do idiota: o processo de idiossubjetivação". Durante a entrevista, Casara afirmou que "a idiossubjetivação é uma ferramenta usada pelo neoliberalismo para formatar pessoas que naturalizam o absurdo".
Casara explicou que o conceito de idiossubjetivação se refere a um processo no qual o sujeito se torna incapaz de perceber que está agindo contra os próprios interesses. "O sujeito idiossubjetivo se torna incapaz de perceber que ele está atuando no sentido contrário aos seus interesses, aos interesses dos filhos, e não faltam exemplos de pessoas que cometem esse vício de pensamento, muito mais próximo do vazio de pensamento do que de reflexão", disse.
Ele destacou que este é um fenômeno global, associado a uma mudança subjetiva provocada pelo que alguns autores denominam de "racionalidade hegemônica, racionalidade neoliberal". Casara mencionou que a motivação para escrever seu livro foi tentar entender por que tantas pessoas aceitam como naturais convicções que, até dez anos atrás, seriam inaceitáveis, ao ponto de eleger representantes dessa barbárie como presidentes.
Para combater o processo de idiossubjetivação, Casara sugere uma abordagem firme: “Não se negocia com vidas, não se pode aproveitar de uma desgraça, de uma tragédia que é política, ecológica, ambiental para que isso venha a ser percebido pelo sujeito como oportunidade de levar uma vantagem.”
Assista:
en_title: " Idiossubjectivation is a tool used by neoliberalism to format people who naturalize absurdity,” says judge
en_content:
The law judge of the Court of Justice of Rio de Janeiro (TJ-RJ) and writer Rubens Casara discussed, in an interview with Giro das Onze, TV 247, his book "The construction of the idiot: the process of idiossubjectivation". During the interview, Casara stated that "idiossubjectivation is a tool used by neoliberalism to format people who naturalize absurdity".
Casara explained that the concept of idiossubjectivation refers to a process in which the subject becomes unable to realize that he is acting against his own interests. "The ill-subjective subject becomes unable to realize that he is acting in the opposite direction to his interests, the interests of his children, and there are no examples of people who commit this vice of thought, much closer to the emptiness of thought than of reflection," he said.
He highlighted that this is a global phenomenon, associated with a subjective change caused by what some authors call "hegemonic rationality, neoliberal rationality". Casara mentioned that the motivation to write her book was to try to understand why so many people accept as natural convictions that, up to ten years ago, would be unacceptable, to the point of electing representatives of this barbarism as presidents.
To combat the process of idiossubjectivation, Casara suggests a firm approach: “You don’t deal with lives, you can’t take advantage of a disgrace, a tragedy that is political, ecological, environmental so that it will be perceived by the subject as an opportunity to take an advantage. ”
Watch:
en_subtitle: Judge Rubens Casara discusses in an interview with TV 247 the impact of neoliberalism on subjective formation of individuals

title: Brasil deve fechar 2023/24 com exportação recorde de café
subtitle: Volume representa uma forte recuperação na comparação com a temporada anterior
content:
A exportação total de café do Brasil deverá fechar em um volume recorde no ano comercial 2023/24 (julho/junho), afirmou nesta quarta-feira o diretor comercial da Eisa, um dos cinco maiores exportadores de café do país, enquanto o diretor-geral do Conselho de Exportadores de Café (Cecafé) também indicou que o país caminha em direção a uma nova máxima, preenchendo espaços deixados no mercado global pela menor oferta de países como Vietnã e Indonésia.
Em entrevista à Reuters no intervalo do Seminário Internacional do Café, em Santos, o diretor comercial da Eisa, Carlos Alberto Santana, estimou a exportação total de café do país, maior produtor e exportador global, em cerca de 46,3 milhões de sacas de 60 kg em 23/24.
Esse volume representa uma forte recuperação na comparação com a temporada anterior, quando os embarques somaram 35,6 milhões de sacas, após o Brasil sofrer com duas safras baixas atingidas por seca e geadas, segundo dados do Cecafé.
O aumento de mais de 10 milhões de sacas de um ano para outro deverá acontecer em meio a "preços excelentes" que impulsionaram negócios de produtores, que estão vendendo seus estoques. As cotações globais vêm sendo sustentadas por menor oferta em outros importantes países produtores.
"O Brasil, na nossa opinião, vai ter um recorde de exportação na safra 2023/24, ainda tem junho... achamos que vai ser a maior exportação da história do Brasil... consequência de estoques que vinham sendo carregados no Brasil, sobretudo na mão do produtor", afirmou o diretor da Eisa, subsidiária do grupo suíço Ecom, que tem 175 anos de história.
No acumulado da safra 2023/24 até abril, a exportação total de café do Brasil soma 39,3 milhões de sacas, ante 39,9 milhões de sacas do mesmo período de 2020/21, ano que até o momento detém a máxima histórica, com 45,6 milhões de sacas no ano fechado, segundo dados do Cecafé.
Santana lembrou que a estrutura do mercado hoje, "invertido", não permite que o exportador carregue estoques de café. E afirmou que a nova safra de café do Brasil, que começou a ser colhida, deverá ser "ligeiramente maior" do que a anterior, o que dá indicação de que as exportações seguirão robustas e tranquilidade para produtores que estão vendendo estoques.
O diretor-geral do Cecafé, Marcos Matos, citou em entrevista à Reuters as exportações recordes de café canéforas (conilon e robusta) brasileiros, com o Brasil ganhando mercado do Vietnã e Indonésia, à medida que sua safra tem crescido nos últimos anos.
"Arábica, conilon, que vai entrar na história, está todo mundo crescendo", afirmou ele, lembrando que desde outubro as exportações brasileiras estão fortes.
Ele lembrou que em relação ao ano recorde anterior (2020/21) a exportação brasileira ainda está um pouco abaixo, "mas acontece que estamos acelerados, então é possível um novo recorde".
Matos disse que agora será preciso acompanhar a movimentação do porto de Santos, o principal para escoamento de café do Brasil, e as condições logísticas, que muitas vezes atrasam os embarques.
"Mas o que é interessante é que estamos em patamares (de exportação) de safra recorde. Ou empata com 20/21 -- vai depender de como será o últimos dois meses --, mas é bem provável que vivamos o nosso melhor momento, se a bola vai entrar ou vai bater na trave, não temos como saber, porque vai depender do porto."
Participantes do mercado reunidos no seminário citaram também a forte demanda pelo café do Brasil, não somente da China, mas de outros países asiáticos, que são novos mercados, para o impulso das exportações.
en_title: Brazil should close 2023/24 with coffee record export
en_content:
Brazil's total coffee exports should close in a record volume in the commercial year 2023/24 (July/June), said on Wednesday Eisa's commercial director, one of the five largest coffee exporters in the country, while the director-general of the Coffee Exporters Council (Cecafé) also indicated that the country walks towards a new maximum, filling spaces left in the global market by the lowest offer of countries such as Vietnam and Indonesia.
In an interview with Reuters at the interval of the International Coffee Seminar in Santos, Eisa's commercial director, Carlos Alberto Santana, estimated the total export of coffee from the country, the largest producer and global exporter, at about 46.3 million bags of 60 kg in 23/24.
This volume represents a strong recovery in comparison with the previous season, when shipments totaled 35.6 million bags, after Brazil suffered with two low harvests hit by drought and frosts, according to data from Cecafé.
The increase of more than 10 million bags from one year to another should happen amid "excellent prices" that have driven producers' businesses, which are selling their stocks. Global quotes have been supported by lower supply in other important producing countries.
"Brazil, in our opinion, will have an export record in harvest 2023/24, still has June... we think it will be the largest export in Brazil's history... resulting from stocks that were being loaded in Brazil, especially in the producer's hand," said Eisa, a subsidiary of the Swiss group Ecom, who has 175 years of history.
In the accumulated harvest 2023/24 until April, the total export of coffee from Brazil adds 39.3 million bags, up to 39.9 million bags of the same period from 2020/21, year that until now holds the historical maximum, with 45.6 million bags in the closed year, according to data from Cecafé.
Santana recalled that the structure of the market today, "inverted", does not allow the exporter to carry coffee stocks. And he said that Brazil's new coffee crop, which began to be harvested, should be "lightly larger" than the previous one, which indicates that exports will follow robust and tranquillity for producers who are selling stocks.
The director-general of Cecafé, Marcos Matos, quoted in an interview with Reuters the record exports of Brazilian coffee (conilon and robust) with Brazil gaining market in Vietnam and Indonesia, as its crop has grown in recent years.
"Arabica, conilon, which will enter history, is everyone growing", he said, recalling that since October Brazilian exports are strong.
He recalled that in relation to the previous record year (2020/21) the Brazilian export is still a little below, "but it happens that we are accelerated, so it is possible a new record".
Matos said that now it will be necessary to follow the movement of the port of Santos, the main for coffee disposal of Brazil, and the logistic conditions, which often delay shipments.
" But what's interesting is that we're on record crop (export) levels. Or tie with 20/21 -- it will depend on how it will be the last two months -- but it is quite likely that we live our best time, whether the ball will enter or will hit the brake, we do not have to know, because it will depend on the port. "
Market participants gathered in the seminar also cited the strong demand for Brazil's coffee, not only from China, but from other Asian countries, which are new markets, for the impulse of exports.
en_subtitle: Volume represents a strong recovery in comparison with previous season

title: “Decisão pela absolvição de Moro revela que o judiciário é um ator central da política”, diz Jeferson Miola
subtitle: Em entrevista à TV 247, jornalista analisa interesses por trás da decisão do TSE
content:
O jornalista Jeferson Miola discutiu em entrevista ao programa Giro das Onze, da TV 247, a recente decisão do Tribunal Superior Eleitoral (TSE) de manter o mandato do senador Sérgio Moro. Segundo Miola, "a absolvição de Moro interessava tanto ao Bolsonaro quanto ao governo Lula".
Miola afirmou que o ex-presidente Jair Bolsonaro atuou pessoalmente para garantir a absolvição de Moro, orientando o Partido Liberal (PL) a não recorrer da decisão do TSE e fazendo contatos com juízes. "Essa sinalização do Bolsonaro é de reaproximação e de reconfiguração da aliança das extremas direitas bolsonarista e lavajatista. Ele solda essa aliança com base nesta atitude de defesa do Moro, tanto que o Sérgio Moro agradeceu ao Bolsonaro", disse Miola.
Para o governo Lula, Miola sugeriu que a manutenção do mandato de Moro evitaria uma possível "martirização" do ex-juiz, que poderia ser vista como uma perseguição judicial e trazer insegurança sobre quem poderia substituí-lo no Senado. "A cassação do Moro não traria nenhuma segurança de que no lugar dele fosse eleito no estado do Paraná, algum político ou senador caninamente mais raivoso e mais ofensivo que o Moro, seria ruim para o próprio governo", afirmou.
Miola observou que a decisão do TSE reflete o papel central do judiciário na política brasileira.
